quick_facts.label: Indian occupational classification
quick_facts.value: National Career Service (Ministry of Labour & Employment) lists the closely aligned occupation “Digital Marketing or Social Media Manager” under NCO 2015 code 2432.0201.

quick_facts.label: Core remit
quick_facts.value: The role owns digital and social-media strategy and supervises its execution; current employer examples also cover integrated campaigns, demand generation, search visibility, content, agencies, measurement and ROI.

quick_facts.label: Education and experience are employer-specific
quick_facts.value: NCS describes a marketing, advertising, BBA-marketing or related graduate background as preferable and states that graduates commonly need 2–5 years’ experience. A July 2026 Sun Pharma posting instead sought an MBA/PGDM in marketing or a related field and 7–10 years in digital marketing, illustrating the higher bar in some large-employer manager roles.

quick_facts.label: Relevant national skills reference
quick_facts.value: An NSDC qualification-pack booklet lists “Digital Marketing Manager” (QP code 2022/ME/MESC/05446) at NSQF Level 6, with 840 total notional hours and the Media & Entertainment Skill Council named as awarding body.

quick_facts.label: Compensation evidence should not be treated as a standard rate
quick_facts.value: Available public estimates differ materially: Indeed’s India tracker reported ₹3,20,210 annual average base pay from 340 reported salaries on February 21, 2026, while a June 2026 IIM Bangalore Manager–Marketing vacancy stated ₹14.02 lakh per annum as indicative remuneration for that distinct, senior contractual role. Title, employer, location, sector, scope and experience therefore matter substantially.

sections.heading: What a Digital Marketing Manager is accountable for
paragraphs: At manager level, the job is not limited to posting content or buying media. The central responsibility is to design the overall digital and social-media marketing strategy, then supervise implementation for the relevant product or business. This makes the role responsible for connecting channel activity to marketing objectives rather than treating channels as separate tasks.

A current India employer example expands that remit into full-funnel campaign planning, demand generation, always-on social campaigns, creative and influencer-agency coordination, SEO/search visibility, campaign and sales measurement, ROI analysis, dashboarding, and cross-functional work with brand and regional teams. The exact channel mix will vary by employer and sector.
bullets: Set strategy, objectives, audience approach, channel priorities and campaign plans.
Coordinate specialists and external creative, media, social or influencer agencies; clarify briefs, timelines and deliverables.
Oversee content, paid-media, search, social, lifecycle or demand-generation activity as applicable to the business.
Monitor campaign, sales and search-performance data; interpret results, report ROI and make optimisation decisions.
Align digital work with brand, sales, regional, product and other stakeholder teams.

sections.heading: Common routes from specialist work into management
paragraphs: For this canonical node, the practical route is usually to first build evidence of execution as a Digital Marketing Specialist or in closely related channel roles, then take responsibility for integrated campaigns, budgets, reporting, vendors and stakeholder coordination. NCS says graduates commonly require 2–5 years of experience for its aligned profile, but this is indicative rather than an eligibility rule.

A relevant undergraduate degree can help, but it is not the only route. NCS identifies marketing, advertising, BBA-marketing and related graduate study as preferable, alongside certified digital-marketing training. More senior employer postings may seek postgraduate marketing qualifications and considerably deeper experience: Sun Pharma’s July 2026 posting requested an MBA/PGDM or related qualification and 7–10 years’ experience.
bullets: Build a demonstrable portfolio: campaign briefs, channel plans, creative examples, dashboards, performance results and lessons learned; protect employer/client-confidential information.
Develop from individual-channel execution into cross-channel planning and ownership of measurable commercial or brand outcomes.
Use a recognised training pathway where useful; the NSDC booklet includes a Digital Marketing Manager qualification-pack reference at NSQF Level 6.
Read each vacancy closely: degree requirements, years of experience, channel emphasis and people/agency-management expectations are not uniform.

sections.heading: Skills to develop
paragraphs: The foundational capability is strategic digital marketing: translating an audience and business objective into an integrated plan, guiding execution and monitoring trends in digital media and social networks. NCS also identifies online brand/product-campaign management, problem solving, teamwork and strong communication as relevant competencies.

Manager-level work requires analytical judgement alongside creative and channel knowledge. Current employer evidence points to performance and sales measurement, ROI analysis, dashboards, search and content performance, agency management and collaboration across teams. AI tools may be part of modern workflows, but should be used within the employer’s governance, brand and data requirements.
bullets: Strategy and planning: audience segmentation, funnel design, channel selection, briefing and prioritisation.
Performance analysis: KPIs, campaign and sales metrics, ROI analysis, dashboards and insight-led optimisation.
Channel fluency: social media, paid campaigns, SEO/search, content, email or demand generation according to the role.
Leadership and delivery: project management, vendor/agency coordination, stakeholder communication and team collaboration.
Professional judgement: problem solving, clear written and verbal communication, trend monitoring and responsible use of emerging tools.

sections.heading: Where and how the work is done
paragraphs: Digital Marketing Managers can work in in-house marketing teams, agencies or sector-specific organisations. The working pattern is shaped by the organisation and campaign needs, not the title alone. For example, Sun Pharma’s July 2026 Mumbai role involved APAC campaign activity and management of creative, media and influencer agencies, while IIM Bangalore’s 2026 marketing-manager brief combined digital campaigns with stakeholder outreach and online/offline events.

NCS’s automotive-sector profile describes field work, local travel and possible overtime, but those details should not be assumed for every Digital Marketing Manager vacancy. Candidates should confirm location, travel, remote/hybrid policy, hours, client-facing expectations and whether the job manages employees, agencies or both before applying.
bullets: Typical collaborators include brand, sales, regional, product, admissions or business teams, plus creative, media and influencer agencies.
Work may combine planning and review meetings with hands-on campaign oversight, reporting and external-partner coordination.
Travel and attendance requirements can be material in sector-specific roles; verify them from the actual employer posting.

sections.heading: Progression beyond this role
paragraphs: Progression is commonly based on a demonstrated ability to move from managing channels and campaigns to owning larger portfolios, teams, budgets and business outcomes. NCS presents its pathway as indicative rather than prescriptive, listing Digital Marketing or Social Media Manager progressing to Digital Marketing or Social Media In-charge and then General Manager.

To progress, build evidence that links marketing activity to outcomes: improved discoverability, qualified demand, conversion, retention, revenue contribution or brand objectives, as appropriate. Experience leading agencies and collaborating across functions is especially relevant because senior roles are accountable for coordination and decisions, not only channel execution.
bullets: Digital Marketing Specialist or channel specialist → Digital Marketing Manager.
Broader marketing/digital lead or in-charge responsibility → general-management or senior marketing leadership roles, subject to employer structure.
Potential lateral specialisations include performance marketing, growth/demand generation, e-commerce, marketing operations, CRM/lifecycle marketing or brand digital strategy.

sections.heading: Compensation context in India
paragraphs: Pay for this title is highly variable and public trackers are not a substitute for a specific offer. As a dated signal only, Indeed reported an India average base salary of ₹3,20,210 per year from 340 reported salaries, updated February 21, 2026. That figure may reflect a different mix of cities, employers, experience levels and job scopes than a senior corporate management vacancy.

For comparison rather than a market benchmark, IIM Bangalore advertised a Manager–Marketing role in June 2026 with indicative remuneration of ₹14.02 lakh per annum plus benefits; it required a master’s degree and at least 10 years’ post-master’s experience. The role is adjacent rather than identical to Digital Marketing Manager, so it demonstrates how seniority and remit can change compensation rather than establishing a salary for this node.
bullets: Evaluate compensation using the dated offer, fixed versus variable pay, benefits, city, level of responsibility, team/agency scope and required experience.
Do not use a single salary website or an older occupational-profile estimate as a guaranteed salary expectation.
Ask whether the stated amount is base pay, CTC, fixed compensation, incentive-inclusive pay or a contractual consolidated amount.
